close_reason: Style: String vs Box<str> is negligible — String is idiomatic
description: secretx-env/src/lib.rs:23 The 'var' field is set once in from_uri and never mutated. Box<str> would shave one usize per instance (no capacity field) and signal immutability at the type level. There is no measurable performance impact at the scale of secretx (one EnvBackend per secret, kept for the process lifetime). Filed as opinion-tier — a serious Rust practitioner would not flag this in code review, but it is the kind of tightening that an idiomatic-style pass would mention.
title: var: String could be Box<str> (taste, not blocking)
